mnemonics: Think of importing like a phonebook. You look up a class (a person) by its full name (package + class name) so you can use it (call them) without having to remember the full address every time.
keyPoints: An import statement provides a shortcut to use a class without its fully qualified name.
The `import` keyword is used to bring classes or entire packages into scope.
The wildcard `*` imports all classes from a package but not its sub-packages.
commonMistakes: Trying to import classes from the `java.lang` package (like `String` or `System`); it is imported automatically.
Using wildcard imports (`.*`) for two packages that contain classes with the same name, leading to ambiguity.
Forgetting that the import statement does not include sub-packages; each level must be imported separately.
